title: Black Holes
puzzle: What exactly is a black hole if you cannot see it directly?
insight: A black hole is a region where gravity becomes so intense that not even light can escape once it crosses the event horizon.
key_ideas: Black holes often form after massive stars collapse.
They can still be detected through nearby matter and motion.
Supermassive black holes sit at the centers of many galaxies.
mind_blower: A black hole is not a hole punched into space. It is a place where spacetime bends so extremely that escape stops making sense.
next_curiosity: If black holes trap light, how did astronomers manage to create an image of one?
narration_text: What exactly is a black hole if you cannot see it directly? A black hole is a region where gravity becomes so intense that not even light can escape once it crosses the event horizon. Black holes often form after massive stars collapse. They can still be detected through nearby matter and motion. Supermassive black holes sit at the centers of many galaxies. A black hole is not a hole punched into space. It is a place where spacetime bends so extremely that escape stops making sense. If black holes trap light, how did astronomers manage to create an image of one?
